description: Phase 1 of the 5-phase LlmRunner hygiene roadmap. Adds cleanup_session as a trait method with no-op default, implements it on ClaudeRunner (delete ~/.claude/projects/<encoded>/<uuid>.jsonl) and GrokRunner (remove_dir_all ~/.grok/sessions/<encoded>/<uuid>/, never touch prompt_history.jsonl). Promotes cleanup_title_artifact_sync to runner.rs as cleanup_claude_session_artifact (returns Result). Adds session_id: Option<Uuid> to RunnerResult. Wires dispatch to call cleanup_session unconditionally post-spawn for every iteration, with one-banner-per-process rate-limit on errors. Removes the opt-in cleanup_title_artifact: bool field from RunnerOpts and all 11 call sites. Adds FakeRunner test seam + tests/runner_cleanup.rs integration tests. Places WORKAROUND() comment markers so future upstream-fix removal is a one-grep operation.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` output
Rust: Scoped tests for touched files pass with `cargo test`
No new .unwrap() or .expect() in production code paths (existing #[cfg(test)] allow continues)
All eprintln! warnings include enough context to identify the failed operation (provider, path, error kind)
No detached threads, no tokio::spawn, no async cleanup — sync only
Comments explain WHY (non-obvious constraint), never narrate WHAT — the WORKAROUND(...) markers carry the explanation
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

mergeStrategy: Merge to main after MILESTONE-FINAL passes. Squash optional.

priorityPhilosophy.description: Hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Anticipate cleanup edge cases (NotFound silent, PermissionDenied banner, missing working_dir, parallel slots, mtime tiebreaker)

2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — cleanup_session is the first capability-aware trait method; the FakeRunner seam will be reused by Phases 2-5 of the hygiene roadmap; explicit-call shape soaks before Phase 5 RAII layers on top
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — pragmatic, reliable, wired in per PRD
4. CORRECTNESS — Compiles, type-checks, scoped tests pass deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY — Clean code, qualityDimensions satisfied, no warnings
6. POLISH — Docs, formatting, CLAUDE.md cleanup_session subsection
principles: Cleanup is a trait method, not an opt-in flag — removing the degree of freedom is the fix
Cleanup targets only the exact (session_id, cwd) tuple — never enumerate-and-sweep
NotFound on artifact path is silent success — future-proof against upstream fix
Grok's per-cwd prompt_history.jsonl is NEVER deleted — accumulates by design
Cleanup runs unconditionally post-spawn — even on spawn failure, because the artifact may still have been written
CLEANUP_WARN_ONCE rate-limits to one banner per process — operator stderr stays readable
WORKAROUND(<provider>-<short-issue>) markers must exist exactly where each cleanup lives so future upstream-fix removal is a one-grep operation
Sync cleanup only — no detached threads, no tokio::spawn, no async cleanup (detached threads don't survive parent CLI exit per learning [2674])
Boundary with coherence-refactoring effort: cleanup_session hook stays single-purpose; engine field-removal leaves clean seams; runner.rs is a peer of future TaskLifecycle, not a parent

prohibitedOutcomes: Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying content
Tests that hand-build HashMaps as RunnerOpts mock state — use the real RunnerOpts struct so wrong field names fail at compile time
Enumerate-and-sweep cleanup (read_dir + glob) — must delete ONLY the (session_id, cwd) tuple this spawn created, never the shared dir
Deleting Grok's per-cwd ~/.grok/sessions/<encoded>/prompt_history.jsonl — that file accumulates across sessions by design

Adding status, lifecycle, reconciliation, or <task-status> logic inside the dispatch post-spawn cleanup hook — task lifecycle belongs to the coherence-refactoring effort; this PRD's hook stays single-purpose
Tidying adjacent code in engine.rs at the field-removal sites (lines 656, 2587) — the coherence Phase 1 carve owns those seams; this PRD only deletes the cleanup_title_artifact: true line
Reimplementing dash-encoding for the Claude artifact path — reuse encoded_cwd_dir at claude.rs:51
.unwrap() in any cleanup path — use match or ? on Result; errors propagate to dispatch which handles the banner
Detached threads / tokio::spawn / async cleanup — sync only; detached threads don't survive parent CLI exit (learning [2674])
Banner spam — every cleanup error must be gated by CLEANUP_WARN_ONCE (single AtomicBool::swap with Relaxed ordering)
Generating the taskPrefix manually in the JSON — task-mgr init auto-generates it; setting it by hand can cause prefix collisions
Refactoring other RunnerOpts fields (db_dir, signal_flag, timeout) under cover of this PRD — they encode genuine per-caller policy and are out of scope

priorityGuidelines.medium: 21-50: Fix in current phase if time permits

acceptanceCriteria: grep -rn 'cleanup_title_artifact' src/ tests/ produces a complete enumeration that matches PRD §6 line numbers (or notes the deltas)
Verify src/loop_engine/runner.rs:166 still declares pub cleanup_title_artifact: bool, runner.rs:222 declares the LlmRunner trait, runner.rs:102 declares RunnerResult, runner.rs:877 declares dispatch
Verify src/loop_engine/runner.rs:327 contains cleanup_title_artifact.then(|| { Uuid::new_v4() }) gate (per PRD §6: NOT in claude.rs)
Verify src/loop_engine/runner.rs:422 contains the inline cleanup_title_artifact_sync(uuid, working_dir) call (per PRD §6: NOT in claude.rs)
Verify src/loop_engine/claude.rs:51 declares encoded_cwd_dir (to be reused for Claude artifact path; do NOT re-implement)
Verify src/loop_engine/claude.rs:739 declares static CLEANUP_WARN_ONCE: AtomicBool with file-private visibility
Verify src/loop_engine/claude.rs:756 declares pub(crate) fn cleanup_title_artifact_sync (the helper to promote)
Confirm urlencoding crate availability for Grok percent-encoding — if not in Cargo.toml, flag that FEAT-002 needs to add it
Write Consumer Impact Table to tasks/progress-<prefix>.txt with one row per cited site (file:line, usage, impact, mitigation) — flag any deltas vs PRD §6
userStories.description: PRD §6 'Consumers of Changed Behavior' lists 17 specific file:line sites that will be modified by Phase 1 (8 production cleanup_title_artifact:true call sites, 3 test sites in grok_runner_unit.rs, 6 test sites in claude.rs lines 2948-3138, the inline UUID gate at runner.rs:327, and the inline cleanup call at runner.rs:422). This task re-greps current main and confirms (or flags drift in) every line citation, then writes a Consumer Impact Table to the progress file. No code changes.
userStories.notes:
Read actual code; never infer line numbers from PRD alone. The PRD was written 2026-05-19 and main may have drifted by a line or two due to merges. The architect's review already populated the Consumer Impact Table in PRD §6 — this task verifies it's still accurate, doesn't reinvent it.

Learning [810]: grep for struct field locations across all construction sites — verify completeness across both src/ and tests/.
Learning [1581]: adding a new spawn_claude param requires updating many mechanical sites — this is the inverse (removal); same discipline applies.
qualityDimensions: Read every cited file:line in the PRD §6 table — never infer from variable names
Output is a Consumer Impact Table in the progress file with concrete file:line citations and impact (OK / BREAKS / NEEDS_REVIEW)
Flag any drift between PRD line numbers and current main — the implementing FEATs will need updated coordinates
userStories.title: Verify Consumer Impact Table matches current source state

acceptanceCriteria: Test: default LlmRunner::cleanup_session impl returns Ok(()) for a stub runner that does NOT override the method (uses a #[cfg(test)] FakeNoOpRunner)
Test: grok_encoded_session_dir(Path::new('/home/user/repo'), Path::new('/home/user')) returns <home>/.grok/sessions/%2Fhome%2Fuser%2Frepo/
Test: grok_encoded_session_dir trailing-slash input normalizes the same as no-slash input
Test: cleanup_claude_session_artifact returns Ok(()) when the target file exists and is removed
Test: cleanup_claude_session_artifact returns Ok(()) when the target file does NOT exist (NotFound is silent success)
Test: cleanup_claude_session_artifact returns Err(TaskMgrError::IoError-like variant) on PermissionDenied (use a read-only parent dir)
Test: cleanup_claude_session_artifact only touches the deterministic UUID-named jsonl; bystander jsonl files in the same encoded_cwd_dir are preserved
Test fixtures derive paths from real Path / PathBuf — never invented string-typed maps; structural assertion: PathBuf::from(grok_encoded_session_dir(...)).is_absolute()
Test file compiles (may be #[ignore] until the impls land in FEAT tasks)
userStories.description: TDD foundation for FEAT-001/-002/-003. Adds unit tests in runner.rs::tests covering: (a) LlmRunner default cleanup_session no-op impl returns Ok(()); (b) grok_encoded_session_dir produces the observed on-disk path (%-percent-encoded cwd under <home>/.grok/sessions/); (c) cleanup_claude_session_artifact (promoted helper) returns Ok(()) on success and on NotFound, returns Err(IoError) on other IO errors.
edgeCases: NotFound on artifact path (artifact never written) → Ok(()) silently, no banner emitted
PermissionDenied (read-only mount) → Err returned; banner is dispatch's responsibility, not the helper's
Worktree path with whitespace (e.g. /tmp/my dir) → encoding produces a deterministic path; helpers handle the literal

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Helper short-circuits returning Ok(()) (best-effort; matches existing cleanup_title_artifact_sync line 757-760 behavior pre-promotion)

invariants: Promoted helper signature returns Result<(), TaskMgrError> — NEVER () (PRD FR-002)
grok_encoded_session_dir is a pure function: same input → same output, no FS reads
userStories.notes:
TDD: tests first. Tests live in src/loop_engine/runner.rs::tests module (alongside the existing CLAUDE_BINARY_MUTEX / GROK_BINARY_MUTEX pattern). For the PermissionDenied case, follow the existing pattern at src/loop_engine/claude.rs:3121-3138 (uses tempdir + fake $HOME).

Known-bad discriminator: a test that PASSES with a stub returning Ok(()) but FAILS the actual contract — assert the FILE WAS REMOVED post-call, not just that Ok was returned.

Learning [2919]: integration test mirrors unit test shape for consistency. Learning [2939]: multi-step visibility widening — the promoted helper will be runner.rs-private; the tests must live in the same file to access it.
qualityDimensions: Use real Path / PathBuf — never hand-built string maps that match the wrong key format
Each test asserts a specific BEHAVIOR (file removed, Err returned, bystander preserved) — never just 'no panic'
Round-trip tests for encoding helpers verify against the actual on-disk separator (%2F for Grok, - for Claude)
userStories.title: Initial tests: cleanup_session trait default + grok_encoded_session_dir round-trip + promoted helper

acceptanceCriteria: Test: ClaudeRunner::cleanup_session(uuid, cwd) deletes <home>/.claude/projects/<dash-encoded-cwd>/<uuid>.jsonl when present
Test: ClaudeRunner::cleanup_session is idempotent — second call returns Ok(()) (NotFound silent)
Test: ClaudeRunner::cleanup_session preserves unrelated jsonl files in the same encoded_cwd_dir
Test: GrokRunner::cleanup_session(uuid, cwd) recursively deletes <home>/.grok/sessions/<percent-encoded-cwd>/<uuid>/ when present
Test: GrokRunner::cleanup_session preserves prompt_history.jsonl at <home>/.grok/sessions/<percent-encoded-cwd>/prompt_history.jsonl
Test: GrokRunner::cleanup_session preserves OTHER session uuid subdirectories under the same encoded_cwd_dir
Test: GrokRunner::cleanup_session is idempotent — second call returns Ok(()) (NotFound silent)
Test: both impls handle working_dir = current_dir fallback when caller passes the path-resolved cwd (sanity check against the dispatch wiring contract in FEAT-006)
Test fixtures use fake $HOME via tempdir (follows existing pattern at claude.rs:3121-3138)
Test file compiles (#[ignore] is acceptable until FEAT-005 + FEAT-006 land)
userStories.description: TDD foundation for FEAT-005 (Grok session_id capture) and FEAT-006 (the cleanup_session impls on both runners). Adds unit tests in runner.rs::tests asserting that each concrete impl correctly deletes its provider-specific artifact shape and preserves bystanders.
edgeCases: Two concurrent Grok sessions in the same cwd (handled at FEAT-004 via mtime tiebreaker; the cleanup test only needs to verify the targeted uuid is deleted)
Empty encoded_cwd_dir (only the target file exists, nothing else) — cleanup succeeds, then the parent dir is empty but NOT removed (we never rmdir the encoded dir)
Worktree path with whitespace → encoding produces a deterministic path; remove_file / remove_dir_all handle the literal path

failureModes.cause: Cleanup called with a uuid for a session that doesn't exist on disk

invariants: Both impls delete EXACTLY ONE filesystem entry (one file for Claude, one dir for Grok) — never enumerate-and-sweep
Grok's prompt_history.jsonl is NEVER deleted under any cleanup_session call
userStories.notes:
Tests live in src/loop_engine/runner.rs::tests. For prompt_history.jsonl preservation, create the file BEFORE calling cleanup_session, then assert it still exists after. For 'preserves other session uuid subdirectories', create two uuid-named subdirs, clean up only one, assert the other still exists.

Learning [2847]: deterministic UUID for safe cleanup in shared dirs — the deletion targets the (session_id, cwd) tuple ONLY, never enumerate-and-sweep. Learning [1617]: do NOT reuse cleanup_ghost_sessions for ~/.claude/projects/ cleanup — different directory, different bug class. Learning [2674]: detached threads don't survive parent CLI exit; cleanup is sync only.
qualityDimensions: Bystander preservation is the most important property — the test that asserts 'unrelated jsonl files survive' is the only thing protecting interactive Claude sessions during long loop runs
Grok's prompt_history.jsonl preservation test is contractual — accidentally deleting it loses cross-session conversation history
Idempotency tests use the same session_id twice in a row; second call must succeed silently
userStories.title: Initial tests: ClaudeRunner::cleanup_session + GrokRunner::cleanup_session behaviors

acceptanceCriteria: Test: dispatch returns the spawn's RunnerResult unchanged when cleanup_session returns Ok(())
Test: dispatch returns the spawn's RunnerResult unchanged when cleanup_session returns Err — error is logged to stderr, not propagated
Test: a single dispatch call with cleanup Err emits exactly one '[cleanup warn] <provider>: <summary> (<path>)' banner line to stderr
Test: two dispatch calls with cleanup Err emit only ONE banner line total (CLEANUP_WARN_ONCE rate-limit — second call is silent)
Test: dispatch calls cleanup_session even when spawn returns Err (a spawn that errors may still have produced an artifact)
Test: dispatch skips cleanup_session when RunnerResult.session_id is None (e.g., GrokRunner couldn't capture a uuid from pre/post dir diff)
Test: cwd passed to cleanup_session matches opts.working_dir when Some, falls back to std::env::current_dir() when None
Test: provider tag in banner reads 'claude' or 'grok' (sourced from RunnerKind, not hardcoded)
Tests use stderr capture (e.g., gag crate or process_redirect) or a #[cfg(test)] hook on CLEANUP_WARN_ONCE so the rate-limit assertion is deterministic
Test file compiles (#[ignore] is acceptable until FEAT-006 + FEAT-008 land)
userStories.description: TDD foundation for FEAT-006 (dispatch post-spawn cleanup) and FEAT-008 (FakeRunner test seam). Outlines the integration test contract for tests/runner_cleanup.rs: dispatch calls cleanup_session unconditionally post-spawn, regardless of spawn success/failure; banner is gated by CLEANUP_WARN_ONCE; spawn result is never modified by cleanup outcome.
edgeCases: RunnerResult.session_id = None (Grok spawn failed before any artifact was created) → dispatch skips cleanup_session for this iteration
spawn returns Err AND cleanup_session returns Err → dispatch returns the spawn Err; cleanup error is logged via banner
Two consecutive dispatch calls with cleanup Err → only the FIRST emits a banner (CLEANUP_WARN_ONCE)
RunnerOpts.working_dir = None → dispatch uses std::env::current_dir() as cwd for cleanup_session (mirroring claude.rs:1154 fallback)

cleanup_session returns Err with PermissionDenied (read-only ~/.claude or ~/.grok mount) → banner format includes provider tag, full path, and error kind (e.g. '[cleanup warn] claude: permission denied (/fake/home/.claude/projects/.../uuid.jsonl)') — assert via stderr capture that all three fields are present

failureModes.cause: cleanup_session returns Err with a banner-relevant path
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Banner emitted (once), then dispatch returns the spawn result unchanged

invariants: cleanup_session is called once per spawn (success or failure), provided session_id.is_some()
CLEANUP_WARN_ONCE is a single AtomicBool gate; subsequent failures stay silent until process restart
dispatch's return value is determined solely by the spawn outcome — never by cleanup
userStories.notes:
Tests live in the NEW tests/runner_cleanup.rs file. Per learning [2919], mirror the shape of tests/runner_trait_dispatch.rs (existing integration test). Per learning [909] (general project convention), integration tests duplicate setup helpers rather than reaching into pub(crate) — keep test-only fixtures local.

CLEANUP_WARN_ONCE deterministic reset: since static AtomicBool persists across tests in the same process, either (a) serialize banner-tests via a Mutex so the warn-once gate is observed cleanly, or (b) expose a #[cfg(test)] reset helper. Decide during implementation; (a) is simpler.

Coherence-boundary check: dispatch's post-spawn hook stays single-purpose — these tests must NOT assert any status / lifecycle / reconciliation behavior alongside cleanup; if a future test wants to assert that, it belongs in the coherence-refactoring effort's test suite, not here.
qualityDimensions: Banner is rate-limited to one per process — test must assert exactly one stderr line across two failure calls
Spawn result is the source of truth — cleanup outcome NEVER modifies dispatch's return value
Cleanup runs unconditionally post-spawn — both success and failure spawn paths must call cleanup_session
session_id = None short-circuits cleanup — dispatch must NOT panic or call cleanup_session with a placeholder uuid
userStories.title: Initial tests: dispatch post-spawn cleanup wiring (FakeRunner integration shape)

acceptanceCriteria: src/loop_engine/runner.rs declares fn cleanup_claude_session_artifact(session_id: Uuid, cwd: Option<&Path>) -> Result<(), TaskMgrError>
Return type is Result<(), TaskMgrError> — NotFound returns Ok(()), other IO errors return Err
The helper has a WORKAROUND(claude-code-2.1.110-session-stub) comment marker explaining WHY the cleanup exists
CLEANUP_WARN_ONCE is reachable from runner.rs (either pub(crate) at claude.rs:739 or moved into runner.rs)
Old fn cleanup_title_artifact_sync at claude.rs:756 is DELETED (no compatibility shim)

Inline caller at runner.rs:422 (was cleanup_title_artifact_sync(uuid, working_dir)) now reads cleanup_claude_session_artifact(uuid, working_dir).unwrap_or_else(|e| <log via CLEANUP_WARN_ONCE banner>) OR just cleanup_claude_session_artifact(uuid, working_dir).ok() — pick whichever matches what FEAT-006 expects from dispatch (preview: FEAT-006 will replace this entire site, so keep it minimal)
Test imports at claude.rs:3020, :3087, :3121, :3138 updated to reach the new runner.rs symbol (or moved alongside the helper into runner.rs::tests)
TEST-INIT-001 tests for the promoted helper pass
grep -rn 'cleanup_title_artifact_sync' src/ tests/ returns 0 hits (full rename, no shim)
cargo check + cargo clippy -- -D warnings + cargo fmt --check pass
userStories.description: Move src/loop_engine/claude.rs:756 (cleanup_title_artifact_sync, returns ()) to src/loop_engine/runner.rs as a module-private fn cleanup_claude_session_artifact(session_id: Uuid, cwd: Option<&Path>) -> Result<(), TaskMgrError>. Change the return type so dispatch can route errors to the banner. Lift CLEANUP_WARN_ONCE (claude.rs:739) to pub(crate) OR move it into runner.rs alongside the helper (implementer's call — both work; pick the cleaner diff). Update the sole inline caller at src/loop_engine/runner.rs:422 (rename + handle Result). Update the four claude.rs test imports (claude.rs:3020, :3087, :3121, :3138) to reach the new runner.rs location. Add WORKAROUND(claude-code-2.1.110-session-stub) comment marker on the new helper. Keep encoded_cwd_dir at claude.rs:51 unchanged — reuse via use crate::loop_engine::claude::encoded_cwd_dir.
userStories.notes:
The helper's existing body (claude.rs:756-785) is correct — copy verbatim, then change return type. NotFound → Ok(()), other Err → Err(TaskMgrError::IoErrorWithContext { ... }). The banner-emission code at lines 773-783 moves into dispatch (FEAT-006), so this helper just returns Err and lets the caller decide.

Learning [2939]: multi-step visibility widening for internal refactoring — promoting the helper changes visibility from file-private to runner.rs-private. Learning [2736]: rename refactor — update all sites and verify completeness via grep.

Decision required: CLEANUP_WARN_ONCE location. Two options: (a) leave at claude.rs:739 with pub(crate); (b) move to runner.rs alongside the helper. Pick whichever produces a smaller, cleaner diff. The PRD §7 Open Question explicitly leaves this to the implementer.
qualityDimensions: Reuse encoded_cwd_dir at claude.rs:51 — do not re-implement dash-encoding
NotFound is silent success — preserved bit-for-bit from the existing helper at claude.rs:771
WORKAROUND(claude-code-2.1.110-session-stub) marker present so future upstream-fix removal is a one-grep operation
Tests at claude.rs:3020/3087/3121/3138 must continue to pass after the import-path update (per PRD FR-002 Validation)
userStories.title: Promote cleanup_title_artifact_sync to runner.rs as cleanup_claude_session_artifact

acceptanceCriteria: LlmRunner trait gains fn cleanup_session(&self, session_id: Uuid, cwd: &Path) -> Result<(), TaskMgrError> with a no-op default impl returning Ok(())
Rustdoc on cleanup_session documents: target identification (the (session_id, cwd) tuple), idempotency, NotFound-is-success, dispatch-side banner-on-error contract
pub(crate) fn grok_encoded_session_dir(cwd: &Path, home: &Path) -> PathBuf added to runner.rs
Encoding uses urlencoding::encode on the absolute cwd string (e.g., /home/user/repo → %2Fhome%2Fuser%2Frepo)
Cargo.toml lists urlencoding under [dependencies] (add it if not present)
TEST-INIT-001 round-trip tests for grok_encoded_session_dir pass
TEST-INIT-001 default cleanup_session test passes
cargo check + cargo clippy -- -D warnings + cargo fmt --check pass
userStories.description: Two small additive changes in src/loop_engine/runner.rs. (1) Extend the LlmRunner trait at line 222 with `fn cleanup_session(&self, session_id: Uuid, cwd: &Path) -> Result<(), TaskMgrError> { Ok(()) }` — no-op default impl so providers with no headless artifact (future cloud-API runners) need zero cleanup code. Add rustdoc documenting target identification, idempotency, NotFound-as-success, and the dispatch-side banner contract. (2) Add `pub(crate) fn grok_encoded_session_dir(cwd: &Path, home: &Path) -> PathBuf` returning `<home>/.grok/sessions/<urlencoding::encode(absolute cwd)>/`. Add urlencoding to Cargo.toml dependencies if not already present.
userStories.notes:
Trait method placement: insert immediately after fn spawn in the trait body. Document on the trait itself, not on each impl — the default impl carries the contract.

urlencoding: PRD §6 'Dependencies' says 'urlencoding crate is already in use elsewhere; reuse it'. Verify via grep -rn 'urlencoding::' src/ — if absent, add to Cargo.toml [dependencies] with a recent version (1.x).

Learning [2956]: RunnerKind enum dispatch keeps allocation-free; the no-op default impl does NOT introduce dynamic dispatch overhead — the trait method is resolved statically per impl.
qualityDimensions: No-op default impl returns Ok(()) — future cloud-API runners inherit this for free
grok_encoded_session_dir is a pure function: same input → same output, no FS reads, no allocations beyond the returned PathBuf
Rustdoc explains the contract (target identification, idempotency, NotFound semantics) — the contract is the only thing future Phase 2-5 impls will read
userStories.title: Add cleanup_session to LlmRunner trait + grok_encoded_session_dir helper

acceptanceCriteria: RunnerResult struct at runner.rs:102 gains pub session_id: Option<Uuid> field
ClaudeRunner::spawn unconditionally generates Uuid::new_v4(), pushes --session-id <uuid> before -p, and populates RunnerResult.session_id with Some(uuid)
The cleanup_title_artifact.then(|| { ... }) gate at runner.rs:327 is REMOVED — UUID generation is unconditional
ClaudeRunner KEEPS the inline cleanup_claude_session_artifact(uuid, working_dir) call at runner.rs:422 for now (transitional; removed in FEAT-006 when dispatch wires it)
GrokRunner::spawn populates RunnerResult.session_id = None (placeholder until FEAT-004 implements pre/post-spawn dir diff capture)
Test alias _assert_claude_result_is_runner_result at runner.rs:907 updated for the new field
Any other RunnerResult { ... } field-by-name construction site flagged by cargo check is updated with session_id: None (or the appropriate Option<Uuid>)

Existing tests at claude.rs:2948 (test_cleanup_title_artifact_false_omits_session_id) will START FAILING because Claude now always injects --session-id — leave it failing; FEAT-007 deletes/retargets it
cargo check + cargo fmt --check pass; cargo test --lib loop_engine::runner passes the new behaviors

Behavior change documented in progress file: from now on, ClaudeRunner ALWAYS emits --session-id and ALWAYS creates an artifact; cleanup is still happening via the inline call until FEAT-006 moves it to dispatch

consumers.mitigation: Add session_id: Some(cleanup_session_id) (cleanup_session_id is the now-unconditional Uuid generated above)
consumers.usage: ClaudeRunner constructs RunnerResult { ... } at end of spawn

consumers.mitigation: Add session_id: None (capture lands in FEAT-004)
consumers.usage: GrokRunner constructs RunnerResult { ... } at end of spawn

consumers.mitigation: Update for the new field — compile-only assertion, low effort
Leave failing here; FEAT-007 deletes the test
consumers.usage: test_cleanup_title_artifact_false_omits_session_id asserts NO --session-id is present when flag is false

semanticDistinctions.context: Sites that previously set cleanup_title_artifact: true (5 sites)
semanticDistinctions.requiredBehavior: Keep: still generates UUID, emits --session-id, cleanup still fires via inline call until FEAT-006

semanticDistinctions.context: Sites that left cleanup_title_artifact at default false (3 sites: engine.rs:656/2587, prd_reconcile.rs:672)
semanticDistinctions.currentBehavior: No UUID, no --session-id, no cleanup; Claude wrote artifact with random uuid that was never cleaned
semanticDistinctions.requiredBehavior: Now generates UUID, emits --session-id, cleanup fires via inline call until FEAT-006 moves it to dispatch — this is the bug-class fix

userStories.description: Extend pub struct RunnerResult at src/loop_engine/runner.rs:102 with a new field session_id: Option<Uuid>. Update both production construction sites: ClaudeRunner at runner.rs:425 populates Some(uuid) by removing the cleanup_title_artifact.then(||) gate at line 327 — generate Uuid::new_v4() unconditionally and inject --session-id (this is the new always-inject contract; the cleanup that follows it is wired in FEAT-006). GrokRunner at runner.rs:599 populates None for now (Grok capture lands in FEAT-004). Update the test alias _assert_claude_result_is_runner_result at runner.rs:907 for the new field. Search-and-fix every other field-by-name RunnerResult { ... } site (cargo check enforces).
userStories.notes:
Behavior change disclosure: removing the .then(||) gate means Claude always emits --session-id. Previously, the 3 'broken' sites (engine.rs:656, engine.rs:2587, prd_reconcile.rs:672) did NOT emit it and thus didn't create the ai-title artifact in a predictable location. Now they always create one. The inline cleanup at runner.rs:422 still fires for everyone, so the net effect is: all 8 production sites now leak-then-cleanup deterministically (rather than 3 sites leaking with random session ids). This is the intermediate state until FEAT-006 wires unconditional cleanup through dispatch.

Learning [2061]: comprehensive struct field refactoring with zero regressions — same approach here. Learning [795]: replace_all misses instances with different indentation — DO NOT use blind replace_all; let cargo check find every site, then edit each by hand. Learning [658]: adding a field to a struct with single construction site is low-risk — RunnerResult has two prod sites + one test alias, so still low-risk.

Why test 2948 is expected to fail here, not in FEAT-007: that test asserts 'cleanup_title_artifact=false must NOT add --session-id'. After this task, the assertion is wrong. FEAT-007 (the field-removal sweep) deletes this test cleanly. Acceptable transitional state.
qualityDimensions: Follow the existing pattern at runner.rs:425 + :599 for RunnerResult field-by-name construction
Use cargo check as the enforcement mechanism — every construction site fails to compile until updated
Inline cleanup call at runner.rs:422 is preserved for transitional safety (no leak window between FEAT-003 and FEAT-006)
userStories.title: Add session_id: Option<Uuid> to RunnerResult; Claude generates UUID unconditionally

acceptanceCriteria: GrokRunner::spawn captures a pre-spawn HashSet<String> of entry names in <home>/.grok/sessions/<encoded>/
Post-child-wait, GrokRunner::spawn captures a second HashSet<String> and computes the difference
Difference is filtered through Uuid::parse_str — non-uuid entries (e.g. 'prompt_history.jsonl') are skipped
Zero new uuid entries → RunnerResult.session_id = None
Exactly one new uuid entry → RunnerResult.session_id = Some(that uuid)
Multiple new uuid entries → pick the entry whose mtime is closest to spawn-end (last-write-wins); document this heuristic in rustdoc on GrokRunner::spawn
Missing <home>/.grok/sessions/<encoded>/ before spawn (first-ever Grok run for this cwd) → snapshot is empty; capture works the same
TEST-INIT-002 'GrokRunner discovers session id from pre/post dir diff' test passes
cargo check + cargo clippy -- -D warnings + cargo fmt --check pass; cargo test --lib loop_engine::runner passes
userStories.description: Inside GrokRunner::spawn (src/loop_engine/runner.rs:471+), snapshot the entry names in <home>/.grok/sessions/<percent-encoded-cwd>/ as a HashSet<String> BEFORE invoking the child. After child wait, list again and compute the difference. The new entry is this iteration's session id; parse via Uuid::parse_str (skip non-uuid entries like prompt_history.jsonl). Populate RunnerResult.session_id = Some(uuid). Handle edge cases: zero new entries → None; multiple new entries → pick by mtime closest to spawn-end (last-write-wins).
edgeCases: Grok session-id discovery finds zero new entries (spawn failed before grok wrote anything) → session_id = None
Grok session-id discovery finds two new entries (concurrent user opened interactive grok in same cwd) → pick by mtime closest to spawn-end
Missing <home>/.grok/sessions/<encoded>/ pre-spawn (first run for this cwd) → empty 'before' snapshot, normal capture
HOME env var unset → snapshot skipped, session_id = None (best-effort)
qualityDimensions: Single read_dir per snapshot — milliseconds even with hundreds of historical sessions (perf requirement)
Filter via Uuid::parse_str — never assume all entries are uuid-named (prompt_history.jsonl exists)
mtime tiebreaker documented in GrokRunner::spawn rustdoc — concurrent interactive grok in the same cwd is a real hazard
best-effort: missing HOME or read_dir failure produces session_id = None, never panics
userStories.title: Grok session_id capture via pre/post-spawn dir diff

acceptanceCriteria: impl LlmRunner for ClaudeRunner gains fn cleanup_session(&self, session_id: Uuid, cwd: &Path) -> Result<(), TaskMgrError>
ClaudeRunner::cleanup_session body is one line: delegates to cleanup_claude_session_artifact(session_id, Some(cwd))
impl LlmRunner for GrokRunner gains fn cleanup_session(&self, session_id: Uuid, cwd: &Path) -> Result<(), TaskMgrError>
GrokRunner::cleanup_session computes <home>/.grok/sessions/<percent-encoded-cwd>/<uuid>/ via grok_encoded_session_dir and calls std::fs::remove_dir_all
GrokRunner::cleanup_session NotFound is silent success (Ok(())); other IO errors return Err(TaskMgrError::IoErrorWithContext { ... })
GrokRunner::cleanup_session NEVER deletes or modifies <home>/.grok/sessions/<encoded>/prompt_history.jsonl (target path is the uuid subdir only)
GrokRunner::cleanup_session has a WORKAROUND(grok-cli-no-persistence-off) comment marker explaining WHY the cleanup exists
grep -rn 'WORKAROUND(claude-code-2.1.110-session-stub)\|WORKAROUND(grok-cli-no-persistence-off)' src/ returns exactly 2 hits (one per provider)
TEST-INIT-002 tests pass for both impls
cargo check + cargo clippy -- -D warnings + cargo fmt --check pass
userStories.description: Add the concrete impls to the existing impl LlmRunner for ClaudeRunner (runner.rs:242+) and impl LlmRunner for GrokRunner (runner.rs:471+). ClaudeRunner::cleanup_session delegates to the promoted cleanup_claude_session_artifact helper (FEAT-001). GrokRunner::cleanup_session computes grok_encoded_session_dir(cwd, home).join(session_id.to_string()) and calls std::fs::remove_dir_all. NotFound on either is silent success; other IO errors return Err. NEVER touch prompt_history.jsonl. Add WORKAROUND(grok-cli-no-persistence-off) comment marker on the GrokRunner cleanup site.
userStories.notes:
ClaudeRunner impl is one-line delegation — the helper does all the work. GrokRunner impl is ~10 lines: resolve HOME, build path, match remove_dir_all result.

HOME resolution for Grok: pattern matches the existing cleanup_claude_session_artifact helper's HOME-absent behavior — if HOME is unset, return Ok(()) (best-effort skip, not Err). This is consistent with the 'best-effort cleanup must not fail the parent operation' principle (learning [1614]).

Learning [2847]: deterministic UUID for safe cleanup in shared directories — the deletion targets the (session_id, cwd) tuple ONLY. Do NOT enumerate <home>/.grok/sessions/<encoded>/ and delete entries — that would race with concurrent interactive grok sessions.
qualityDimensions: Cleanup deletes ONLY the exact (session_id, cwd) tuple — never enumerate-and-sweep
Grok's per-cwd prompt_history.jsonl is NEVER deleted (cleanup is scoped to the uuid subdirectory only)
WORKAROUND() markers exist exactly where each cleanup lives — future upstream-fix removal is greppable
NotFound-as-silent-success preserved for both providers — future-proof against upstream fix
userStories.title: Implement ClaudeRunner::cleanup_session and GrokRunner::cleanup_session

acceptanceCriteria: dispatch at runner.rs:877 calls runner.cleanup_session(session_id, &cwd) after spawn returns, when result.session_id.is_some()
cwd is derived from opts.working_dir (PathBuf::from) OR std::env::current_dir() fallback when working_dir is None

Cleanup runs even when spawn returns Err (a spawn failure may still have produced an artifact) — OR the dispatch rustdoc explicitly documents that Err-path Claude artifacts are not cleaned because the UUID is not surfaced in the error variant (acceptable best-effort limitation per PRD §6)
On cleanup_session Err: emit '[cleanup warn] <provider>: <summary> (<path>)' to stderr via eprintln!, gated by CLEANUP_WARN_ONCE swap (first error in process prints; subsequent errors stay silent)
Provider tag in banner reads 'claude' or 'grok' (sourced from RunnerKind, NOT hardcoded)
dispatch returns the spawn's original RunnerResult / Err unchanged — cleanup outcome NEVER modifies it
The inline cleanup_claude_session_artifact(uuid, working_dir) call at runner.rs:422 inside ClaudeRunner::spawn is REMOVED in the same commit
After this task, ClaudeRunner::spawn no longer performs cleanup — that's dispatch's job
TEST-INIT-003 tests pass (banner emitted once, dispatch result unchanged, cleanup runs on success AND failure spawns, session_id = None skips cleanup)
cargo check + cargo clippy -- -D warnings + cargo fmt --check pass
cargo test --test runner_cleanup passes (integration test from FEAT-009)

consumers.mitigation: Remove the inline call — cleanup is now dispatch's job. Without this removal, cleanup fires TWICE per Claude iteration (once inline, once in dispatch); the second call gets NotFound (silent), so it would still work but be wasteful + violates 'cleanup belongs to the dispatch boundary' principle.
consumers.usage: Inline cleanup_claude_session_artifact(uuid, working_dir) call inside ClaudeRunner::spawn after child.wait()

consumers.mitigation: Add post-spawn cleanup_session call when session_id.is_some(); error-banner gated by CLEANUP_WARN_ONCE; return value unchanged
consumers.usage: dispatch free fn — current behavior is pass-through (match kind, call spawn, return result)

semanticDistinctions.currentBehavior: Inline cleanup at runner.rs:422 deletes the artifact synchronously after child.wait()
semanticDistinctions.requiredBehavior: Cleanup moves to dispatch — same FS operation, just called from a different stack frame. Net behavior identical (file is deleted post-child).

semanticDistinctions.context: Grok iterations (have session_id only when capture succeeds)
semanticDistinctions.currentBehavior: No cleanup at all — every iteration leaks a session dir
semanticDistinctions.requiredBehavior: Dispatch calls GrokRunner::cleanup_session when session_id.is_some() — bug-class fix for Grok

userStories.description: Modify pub fn dispatch at src/loop_engine/runner.rs:877 so that after runner.spawn(...) returns, it calls runner.cleanup_session(session_id, cwd) if result.session_id.is_some(). Source cwd from opts.working_dir; fallback to std::env::current_dir() when None. Cleanup runs regardless of spawn success/failure. On Err from cleanup_session: emit '[cleanup warn] <provider>: <summary> (<path>)' via eprintln!, gated by CLEANUP_WARN_ONCE. Provider tag from RunnerKind::Display (or inline match). Cleanup failure NEVER changes dispatch's return value. **In the same commit, REMOVE the inline cleanup_claude_session_artifact(uuid, working_dir) call at runner.rs:422 inside ClaudeRunner::spawn** — cleanup is now the dispatch boundary's job, not the runner's. NOTE: This is the modifiesBehavior task — ANALYSIS-001 must have produced the Consumer Impact Table.
qualityDimensions: Cleanup runs unconditionally for headless runs — session_id.is_some() is the gate, not a config flag
CLEANUP_WARN_ONCE swap is wait-free (single AtomicBool::swap with Relaxed ordering)
Cleanup failure NEVER changes dispatch's return value
Provider tag sourced from RunnerKind — adding a third provider in Phase 2-5 only needs to add the cleanup arm + a new tag, not modify the banner format
No .unwrap() in the post-spawn cleanup path — use match or if-let on Result
dispatch hook is single-purpose: cleanup only, no lifecycle/status/reconciliation (coherence-refactoring boundary)
userStories.title: Wire dispatch to call cleanup_session unconditionally post-spawn; remove inline cleanup call

(2) Remove the destructure binding at runner.rs:279 (ClaudeRunner) and runner.rs:489 (GrokRunner). (3) Delete the rustdoc example at runner.rs:137 that includes the field. (4) Drop the field literal at all 8 production call sites: src/loop_engine/engine.rs:656, engine.rs:2587, src/loop_engine/prd_reconcile.rs:672, src/commands/curate/enrich.rs:247, src/commands/curate/mod.rs:638, src/loop_engine/progress.rs:328, src/loop_engine/merge_resolver.rs:260, src/learnings/ingestion/mod.rs:115. (6) Update src/loop_engine/claude.rs tests: DELETE test_cleanup_title_artifact_false_omits_session_id (line 2948); RENAME test_cleanup_title_artifact_true_adds_valid_uuid_v4_session_id (line 2959) → test_spawn_claude_always_injects_session_id and drop the flag from the body. Keep tests at claude.rs:3020, :3087, :3121, :3138 (they target the helper; FEAT-001 already updated their imports).
userStories.notes:
This is the big mechanical sweep. ~10 files, ~14 edits. Single commit so the bisect anchor is clean.

Learning [795] (CRITICAL): replace_all misses instances with different indentation — DO NOT use blind replace_all across the workspace. Edit each site individually after grep enumerates them. Use cargo check as the enforcement loop: removing the field at runner.rs:166 makes every call site fail to compile; walk through each error, edit by hand, recompile, repeat until green.

Learning [2736]: rename refactor — update all sites and verify completeness. The grep -rn at the end is the safety net.

Replacement test for grok_runner_unit.rs: use a tempdir fake $HOME, create <fake_home>/.grok/sessions/<encoded>/<uuid>/some_file.json, call GrokRunner.cleanup_session(uuid, cwd), assert the <uuid>/ dir is gone but the encoded_cwd_dir itself still exists. Mirror the shape of the deleted test for consistency (learning [2919]).

Documentation update required (per PRD §6 Documentation): in tasks/prd-grok-fallback-runner.md:284, drop the 'Grok runner silently ignores the flag' line. Also handles in tasks/grok-fallback-runner.json — but DON'T edit that file directly (it's the source of truth for the loop engine); use sed-like edits only on the markdown PRD.

PRD §7 Open Question 'Tactical patch on main removal sequencing': YES, the 3 sites at engine.rs:656/2587 and prd_reconcile.rs:672 carrying the tactical patch are removed as part of THIS task. Single bisectable commit.
qualityDimensions: grep -rn 'cleanup_title_artifact' src/ tests/ → 0 hits is the success gate
Single commit so bisect can isolate any regression to the sweep
Replacement test in grok_runner_unit.rs exercises the new contract, not just the absence of the old contract

Coherence-refactoring boundary: at engine.rs:656/:2587, drop only the cleanup_title_artifact line — do NOT tidy adjacent SpawnOpts/RunnerOpts construction. Clean seam left for the coherence Phase 1 carve.

acceptanceCriteria: #[cfg(test)] mod with pub(super) struct FakeRunner declared in src/loop_engine/runner.rs
FakeRunner constructor accepts: a pre-built RunnerResult template, an Option<Box<dyn Fn(&Path) + Send + Sync>> artifact-creation closure, an Arc<Mutex<Vec<(Uuid, PathBuf)>>> cleanup recorder
FakeRunner::spawn invokes the artifact-creation closure (if any) BEFORE returning the templated RunnerResult — simulating side effects from the real CLI
FakeRunner::cleanup_session pushes (session_id, cwd.to_path_buf()) onto the recorder Vec, then runs a configurable inner result (Ok or Err — make this part of construction)
FakeRunner is reusable across runner.rs::tests AND tests/runner_cleanup.rs (re-export via pub(crate) or duplicate per learning [909])
Doc comment on FakeRunner explains the seam is #[cfg(test)] only — NOT a production dry-run mode (PRD non-goals §5)
cargo check + cargo clippy -- -D warnings + cargo fmt --check pass
userStories.description: Add a #[cfg(test)] FakeRunner struct in src/loop_engine/runner.rs that impls LlmRunner. Constructor takes (a) configurable spawn output (RunnerResult fields settable per construction), (b) a configurable artifact-creation closure that runs inside spawn (simulating Claude's <uuid>.jsonl write or Grok's directory write), (c) a recordable cleanup-invocation Arc<Mutex<Vec<(Uuid, PathBuf)>>> so tests can assert cleanup_session was called with the expected arguments.
userStories.notes:
Two visibility shapes are workable: (a) pub(crate) in runner.rs::tests-or-similar so tests/runner_cleanup.rs can import via crate::loop_engine::runner::FakeRunner; (b) duplicate FakeRunner in tests/common/ per learning [909] (integration tests duplicate setup helpers rather than reaching into pub(crate)). PRD FR-008 doesn't pick — pick (a) here since the seam is single-purpose and Phase 2-5 will reuse it.

For the artifact-creation closure: typical use is fake-Claude writing a single uuid.jsonl file, or fake-Grok writing a uuid directory containing 4-5 JSON files. The closure receives &Path (the cwd) and must compute the encoded_cwd_dir path itself — exactly how the real CLI would.

Learning [2891]: extract common subprocess scaffolding when adding the second agent implementation — applies to test infrastructure too, not just production code.
qualityDimensions: FakeRunner is #[cfg(test)] only — no production dry-run mode (PRD non-goal)
Recorder is Arc<Mutex<Vec<...>>> for thread-safe assertion across slot-style parallel tests
Artifact-creation closure is opt-in via Option<...> — tests that don't need side effects don't pay the cost
Constructor accepts Result-typed inner cleanup_session outcomes so tests can simulate cleanup failures
userStories.title: Add FakeRunner test seam under #[cfg(test)]

acceptanceCriteria: tests/runner_cleanup.rs file exists with #[test] fns
Test 1 (Claude shape): fake-Claude artifact present after spawn, absent after dispatch returns Ok
Test 2 (Grok shape): fake-Grok artifact directory present after spawn, absent after dispatch returns Ok
Test 3 (banner): single dispatch call with cleanup Err emits exactly ONE '[cleanup warn]' line to stderr; spawn result unchanged
Test 4 (rate-limit): two consecutive dispatch calls with cleanup Err emit exactly ONE banner line total (CLEANUP_WARN_ONCE)
Test 5 (parallel slots, optional but preferred per PRD §7): two FakeRunners running in different cwds produce DISJOINT encoded session dirs — neither test's cleanup touches the other's artifact
Test fixtures use tempdir for fake $HOME / cwd — never real $HOME
Tests mirror the shape of tests/runner_trait_dispatch.rs (learning [2919]) — same setup helpers, same assertion style
cargo test --test runner_cleanup passes
cargo check + cargo clippy -- -D warnings + cargo fmt --check pass
userStories.description: Create the NEW tests/runner_cleanup.rs integration test file. Three tests minimum: (a) fake-Claude single-file artifact — drives dispatch with a FakeRunner whose artifact-creation closure writes a fake <uuid>.jsonl; asserts file exists post-spawn, absent post-dispatch; (b) fake-Grok directory artifact — same shape with a directory artifact; (c) cleanup-failure banner — FakeRunner returns Err from cleanup_session; assert dispatch returns the spawn result unchanged and the banner is emitted exactly once (CLEANUP_WARN_ONCE). Plus optional (d) parallel-slot disjoint dir test (per PRD §7 Open Question — preferred location).
edgeCases: Test isolation: CLEANUP_WARN_ONCE state persists across tests; serialize or reset
Parallel slots: two FakeRunners in different cwds → disjoint encoded session dirs; their cleanups never interfere
Cleanup Err path: dispatch returns the original spawn result, NOT an error wrapping the cleanup failure
userStories.notes:
CLEANUP_WARN_ONCE state is process-global and persists across tests. Two options:
(a) Serialize banner-tests via a Mutex so they run sequentially and observe the warn-once gate cleanly.
(b) Expose a #[cfg(test)] reset helper (e.g., pub(crate) fn reset_cleanup_warn_once_for_test()) and call it at the start of each banner test.
PRD §7 leaves this to the implementer. Pick (a) (simpler) unless the test-order coupling becomes painful.

Learning [909] (general convention): integration tests duplicate setup helpers rather than reaching into pub(crate). Apply by importing FakeRunner from crate::loop_engine::runner::FakeRunner (which is pub(crate)) but writing test-specific helpers inline.

Stderr capture: use the gag crate if available, or set up a custom Write target via a test fixture. Avoid spawning a subprocess just to capture stderr.

Parallel-slot test placement: PRD §7 Open Question 'GrokRunner concurrency under parallel slots' resolves to 'preferred location: tests/runner_cleanup.rs'. Include this test here.
qualityDimensions: Tests drive dispatch end-to-end through FakeRunner — never call cleanup_session directly (defeats the integration test purpose)
tempdir-based fake $HOME / cwd — never touch the real ~/.claude/ or ~/.grok/
Mirror tests/runner_trait_dispatch.rs structure for consistency (learning [2919])
Banner assertion captures stderr deterministically — CLEANUP_WARN_ONCE state is reset or serialized between tests
userStories.title: Add tests/runner_cleanup.rs integration test

Verify GrokRunner::cleanup_session NotFound returns Ok(()) via reading the impl
Verify GrokRunner::cleanup_session targets <home>/.grok/sessions/<encoded>/<uuid>/ ONLY — not the encoded_cwd_dir parent
Verify dispatch post-spawn hook contains ONLY cleanup_session call + banner gating — no status/lifecycle/reconciliation logic
Verify CLEANUP_WARN_ONCE gates the banner (single AtomicBool::swap with Relaxed ordering)
Verify dispatch return value is determined solely by spawn outcome — cleanup outcome never modifies it

Verify all new symbols (cleanup_session, cleanup_claude_session_artifact, grok_encoded_session_dir, FakeRunner, RunnerResult.session_id) have callers in production OR are #[cfg(test)] gated — no orphan-warning
For each issue found: spawn a CODE-FIX-xxx or WIRE-FIX-xxx task via `echo '{...}' | task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by MILESTONE-1`
If no issues found: emit '<task-status>CODE-REVIEW-1:done</task-status>' with one-line 'No issues found' progress note
userStories.description: Review FEAT-001 through FEAT-009 for correctness, security, idiom compliance, and reachability. Focus areas: (1) no .unwrap()/.expect() in production cleanup paths; (2) NotFound is silent success in both impls; (3) Grok's prompt_history.jsonl is NEVER deleted; (4) dispatch hook stays single-purpose (no status/lifecycle/reconciliation seepage — coherence-refactoring boundary); (5) WORKAROUND(claude-code-2.1.110-session-stub) + WORKAROUND(grok-cli-no-persistence-off) markers present and accurate; (6) qualityDimensions for each task satisfied; (7) wiring reachability — dispatch is the only call site for cleanup_session in production, no orphan unused-import warnings on new symbols.
userStories.notes:
Use the rust-python-code-reviewer agent. For each issue spawn one task:
```sh
echo '{
  "id": "CODE-FIX-001",
  "title": "Fix: <specific issue>",
  "description": "From CODE-REVIEW-1: <details>",
  "rootCause": "<file:line + issue>",
  "exactFix": "<specific change>",
  "verifyCommand": "<shell command>",
  "acceptanceCriteria": ["Issue resolved", "No new warnings"],
  "priority": 14,
  "touchesFiles": ["affected/file.rs"]
}' | task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by MILESTONE-1
```

Document findings in tasks/progress-<prefix>.txt. Commit any task additions with `chore: CODE-REVIEW-1 - Add CODE-FIX tasks`.
qualityDimensions: Verify the coherence-refactoring boundary rules in dispatch — no status/lifecycle/reconciliation logic crept in
Verify WORKAROUND markers are exactly where each cleanup lives so future upstream-fix removal is greppable
Verify all new public-ish symbols (pub(crate) in runner.rs) are reachable from production or #[cfg(test)] gated
userStories.title: Code review: cleanup_session contract, error handling, wiring reachability, WORKAROUND markers

grep -rn 'WORKAROUND(claude-code-2.1.110-session-stub)\|WORKAROUND(grok-cli-no-persistence-off)' src/ → 2 hits (PRD Success Metric 'Greppable workarounds')

Fix every failure inline (including pre-existing ones below the ~12-failure threshold).
userStories.notes:
Run full suite ONCE per milestone (not per iteration). Per CLAUDE.md tool usage protocol, pipe all test/build commands to a temp file and grep for results in the same command:
```bash
cargo test 2>&1 | tee /tmp/test-results.txt | tail -3 && grep -E 'FAILED|error\[' /tmp/test-results.txt | head -10
cargo clippy -- -D warnings 2>&1 | tee /tmp/clippy.txt | tail -3 && grep '^error' /tmp/clippy.txt | head -10
```

Pre-existing failures: PRD does not name any specific known-failing tests pre-Phase-1. If ~12 unrelated failures surface, escape hatch is to spawn FIX tasks and BLOCKED — but typical case is full-green.
qualityDimensions: Trunk-green is the invariant this gate exists to protect
Both PRD Success Metrics (zero residual flag, greppable workarounds) verified at the gate
Pre-existing failures within scope; punted only above the ~12-failure threshold via spawned FIX-xxx tasks

Both FakeRunners use distinct fake-cwds (e.g., /tmp/dir-a, /tmp/dir-b inside a tempdir)
After both spawns return, both artifacts exist in their respective encoded session dirs
After dispatch returns for both, each artifact is gone from its own dir AND the other's artifact is untouched
Test uses Arc<Mutex<...>> recorder to verify cleanup_session was called with the correct (session_id, cwd) for each runner
cargo test --test runner_cleanup parallel_slot_disjoint_session_dirs passes

userStories.description: Add a hardening test exercising the PRD §2.5 'Parallel slots running concurrently' edge case AND PRD §6 Risk #1 'Grok pre/post-spawn dir diff races with concurrent interactive grok'. Place in tests/runner_cleanup.rs. Two FakeRunners in distinct (fake-)cwds run their spawn closures simultaneously; assert their encoded session dirs are disjoint AND their cleanups touch only their own (session_id, cwd) tuple.
edgeCases: Two slots running in the same cwd (synthetic — production guarantees distinct worktree cwds) → out of scope; PRD asserts disjoint cwds by construction
Race between snapshot pre-spawn and a concurrent interactive grok writing a new uuid → mtime tiebreaker (covered separately in FEAT-004 unit tests)
userStories.notes:
PRD §6 Risk #1 mitigation says 'Parallel-slot integration test asserts disjoint cwds produce disjoint encoded session dirs'. This is that test.

If FEAT-009 already included this as 'test 5' (optional but preferred), this task can be a no-op completion (`<task-status>TEST-001:done</task-status>` with progress note 'covered by FEAT-009 test 5'). Otherwise add it here.

qualityDimensions: Concurrency test uses real std::thread::spawn — not sequential simulation
Disjoint-dir assertion is the property under test, not just 'no crash'

acceptanceCriteria: Read each of the 8 production call sites and document the working_dir value passed (Some(path) vs None)

This is the PRD's 'cross-boundary integration verification' — single distinct path (cwd → encoded artifact path) traced across module boundaries (caller's module → loop_engine::runner → claude::encoded_cwd_dir or runner::grok_encoded_session_dir → std::fs).

Document the trace in the progress file with the same structure used for Consumer Impact Tables: site, value passed, expected encoded path, verification command. If anything looks wrong, spawn a WIRE-FIX task.

acceptanceCriteria: Read all FEAT-001..9 code + tests in one pass
Spawn REFACTOR-N-xxx tasks for any DRY/complexity/clarity issues found
Verify FakeRunner is reusable for Phase 2-5 (capability discovery, error taxonomy, args builder, RunnerSession RAII) — if not, spawn REFACTOR task to adjust its shape
Verify cleanup_session impls are minimal — no shared scaffolding extracted prematurely (per the Phase 2 Foundation note in PRD §6: explicit-call shape soaks before RAII is layered on top)
For each issue found: spawn REFACTOR-N-xxx via task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by MILESTONE-FINAL
If no issues found: emit '<task-status>REFACTOR-REVIEW-FINAL:done</task-status>' with one-line 'No issues found'
userStories.description: Full-context review of all code + tests added by Phase 1. Look for DRY violations, complexity creep, coupling concerns, clarity issues, pattern adherence. Particular attention to: (a) any shared scaffolding between ClaudeRunner::cleanup_session and GrokRunner::cleanup_session that should be extracted now vs deferred to Phase 2-5; (b) test setup duplication between tests/runner_cleanup.rs and tests/runner_trait_dispatch.rs (per learning [909], duplication is OK across integration tests); (c) the dispatch hook readability — is the post-spawn block obviously single-purpose? (d) FakeRunner ergonomics — would Phase 2-5 want different constructor shapes?
userStories.notes:
Use rust-python-code-reviewer agent. Be RESTRAINED about extracting shared scaffolding — Phase 2-5 will reshape the trait surface anyway; prematurely DRYing cleanup_session impls now adds churn cost without benefit. If a refactor is genuinely valuable (e.g., the dispatch hook is hard to read), spawn the task; otherwise pass.

Learning [2891]: extract common subprocess scaffolding immediately when adding the second agent implementation — applies to spawn scaffolding, NOT necessarily cleanup. Cleanup impls are tiny (1 line for Claude, ~10 for Grok); DRY pressure is low.
qualityDimensions: Restrained extraction — Phase 2-5 will reshape the surface; don't pre-DRY
FakeRunner ergonomics review with Phase 2-5 reuse in mind
Dispatch hook readability: the single-purpose cleanup is obvious at a glance

userStories.description: Authoritative final check before MILESTONE-FINAL. Verifies all PRD §6 Documentation items: docs/designs/runner-trait-hygiene.md exists (already created); src/loop_engine/CLAUDE.md gains a brief subsection (<10 lines) on the cleanup_session contract; LlmRunner trait rustdoc updated with the cleanup_session paragraph; tasks/prd-grok-fallback-runner.md:284 'Grok runner silently ignores the flag' line dropped; tasks/long-term-learnings.md superseded entry recorded for [1626] via `task-mgr learn`. Grep-verified reachability for all new public functions. No .unwrap() in production. Test fixtures use real struct field names.
userStories.notes:
Documentation update required for src/loop_engine/CLAUDE.md — add a brief subsection like:
```
### Session artifact cleanup

Every LlmRunner impl provides `cleanup_session(session_id, cwd)`; dispatch calls it unconditionally post-spawn when `session_id.is_some()`. NotFound is silent success. Errors emit one banner per process (CLEANUP_WARN_ONCE) and never modify the spawn return value. WORKAROUND(claude-code-2.1.110-session-stub) and WORKAROUND(grok-cli-no-persistence-off) markers point at the cleanup sites — future upstream fixes are a one-grep removal.
```

Learning supersession via task-mgr learn:
```sh
task-mgr learn --task-id $PREFIX-VERIFY-001 --outcome workaround --title 'Cleanup as a trait method, called unconditionally by dispatch' --content 'Per-provider session-artifact cleanup belongs to the LlmRunner trait, not an opt-in flag on RunnerOpts. dispatch calls runner.cleanup_session(session_id, cwd) when session_id.is_some(), regardless of spawn success. Supersedes [1626] (opt-in cleanup_title_artifact flag pattern).' --tags runner,trait,cleanup,workaround --confidence high
```

PRD §7 stakeholder review timing: confirm the coherence-refactoring effort's reviewer has seen the diff; document the hand-off (or 'no overlap conflicts found') in the progress file. Open Question can be marked [x] resolved.
